evals.prompt: I want to write an R script that tests whether white matter FA and FW predict 13 different behavioral scores in a Parkinson's disease cohort, across 4 pipeline × model combinations. How should I structure this?
evals.expected_output: Skill guides user through: (1) unit-of-work sentence identifying metric × behavioral-score as the two dimensions, (2) MODES/BEHS/COVS constants block, (3) run_one() pseudocode before any function, (4) function contract table, (5) trivial entry point loop with tryCatch. Should NOT jump straight to writing fit_models() code.

assertions.name: unit-of-work sentence elicited or stated
assertions.description: Skill produces or explicitly solicits a 'I want to run X for every A × B' sentence before proposing code structure

assertions.name: constants block proposed before functions
assertions.description: MODES/BEHS (or equivalent) constants are defined before any function pseudocode or implementation

assertions.name: run_one pseudocode written before function implementations
assertions.description: Orchestrator pseudocode or skeleton appears before the atomic function implementations are drafted

assertions.name: entry point loop is trivial
assertions.description: The outermost loop is shown as a simple nested for loop with error handling — not complex logic

assertions.name: error handling in entry point
assertions.description: tryCatch, try-except, or equivalent wrapper is included in the entry point

evals.prompt: I'm building a Python pipeline that extracts connectivity features from fMRI data. I need to run the same extraction for every subject × session × atlas combination and save the results. There are 3 atlases, 2 sessions per subject, and about 200 subjects. Help me structure this.
evals.expected_output: Skill adapts to a non-statistical use case (feature extraction) and non-R language. Should identify subject × session × atlas as the iteration dimensions, propose constants for the three dimensions, sketch run_one() pseudocode for extraction, and show a Python-idiomatic entry point (nested for loops with try/except). Should not assume lmer or statistical modeling.

assertions.name: adapts to non-statistical use case
assertions.description: Skill does not suggest statistical modeling functions (lmer, fit_models) — it works with the feature extraction framing

assertions.name: uses Python idioms
assertions.description: Code examples use Python syntax (try/except, os.makedirs, f-strings or .format) rather than R syntax

assertions.name: all three dimensions represented in constants
assertions.description: ATLASES (or equivalent), SESSIONS, and some subject-handling strategy appear in the proposed constants

assertions.name: output naming convention proposed
assertions.description: Skill proposes a file naming pattern like subject_session_atlas_type.ext

evals.prompt:
Here's my analysis script — can you clean it up? It's getting unwieldy.

```r
# Load data
df <- read.delim('data/merged.tsv')
df <- df[df$shell == 'multi' & df$softmod != 'dipy-dti', ]
df$pipeline <- factor(df$pipeline)
df$softmod  <- factor(df$softmod)
df$subj     <- factor(df$subj)

# FA ~ UPDRS_1
dat_fa_u1 <- df[complete.cases(df[, c('fa', 'UPDRS_1', 'age', 'sex')]), ]
m_fa_u1   <- lmer(UPDRS_1 ~ fa + age + sex + (1|subj) + (1|roi_labels), data = dat_fa_u1, REML = FALSE)
write.csv(broom.mixed::tidy(m_fa_u1), 'results/fa_UPDRS_1_fixed.csv', row.names = FALSE)
ggsave('results/fa_UPDRS_1_scatter.png', ggplot(dat_fa_u1, aes(fa, UPDRS_1)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method='lm'))

# FA ~ UPDRS_2
dat_fa_u2 <- df[complete.cases(df[, c('fa', 'UPDRS_2', 'age', 'sex')]), ]
m_fa_u2   <- lmer(UPDRS_2 ~ fa + age + sex + (1|subj) + (1|roi_labels), data = dat_fa_u2, REML = FALSE)
write.csv(broom.mixed::tidy(m_fa_u2), 'results/fa_UPDRS_2_fixed.csv', row.names = FALSE)
ggsave('results/fa_UPDRS_2_scatter.png', ggplot(dat_fa_u2, aes(fa, UPDRS_2)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method='lm'))

# FA ~ MOCA
dat_fa_mc <- df[complete.cases(df[, c('fa', 'MOCA', 'age', 'sex')]), ]
m_fa_mc   <- lmer(MOCA ~ fa + age + sex + (1|subj) + (1|roi_labels), data = dat_fa_mc, REML = FALSE)
write.csv(broom.mixed::tidy(m_fa_mc), 'results/fa_MOCA_fixed.csv', row.names = FALSE)
ggsave('results/fa_MOCA_scatter.png', ggplot(dat_fa_mc, aes(fa, MOCA)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method='lm'))
```

(same pattern continues for fw...)
evals.expected_output: Skill identifies the repeating unit (lmer fit for one metric × outcome pair), extracts MODES and BEHS constants, proposes fit_models() and plot_scatter() as atomic functions, separates the ggsave call into a plot function that receives out_path, and proposes run_one() + trivial entry point. Should also note the constants buried in the code (covariates age/sex, data path, output dir).

assertions.name: repeating unit identified
assertions.description: Skill explicitly states the repeating unit as metric × behavioral outcome (or equivalent)

assertions.name: MODES and BEHS extracted as constants
assertions.description: Proposes MODES = c('fa', 'fw') and BEHS with the three outcomes as top-level constants

assertions.name: ggsave separated from fit call
assertions.description: Proposes that the plot be in a separate function from the model fitting

assertions.name: covariates extracted to constants
assertions.description: age and sex (or COVS) are identified as a constant that was buried in the original code

assertions.name: run_one proposed as orchestrator
assertions.description: run_one() or equivalent is proposed to orchestrate the per-pair workflow
